  ### SEQUENCE OBJECTS
```python
from Bio.Seq import Seq
```


```python
my_seq = Seq("GATCG")
```


```python
for index, letter in enumerate(my_seq):
    print("%i %s" % (index,letter))
```

    0 G
    1 A
    2 T
    3 C
    4 G



```python
# We can also print the length of each sequence
print(len(my_seq))
```

    5



```python
# We can access different positions the same way as above and with seek object
print(my_seq[0])
```

    G



```python
print(my_seq[4])
```

    G



```python
print(my_seq[2])
```

    T



```python
# You can count the occurences of specific things
Seq("AAAA").count("AA")
```




    2




```python
my_seq = Seq("GATCGATGGGCCTATATAGGATCGAAAATCGC")
```


```python
# This lets us know the length of the sequence 
len(my_seq)
```




    32




```python
my_seq.count("G")
```




    9




```python
# We can measure the GC content of our sequence
100 * (my_seq.count("G") + my_seq.count("C")) / len(my_seq)
```




    46.875




```python
# This will import a GC function that will give us the fraction of GC
from Bio.SeqUtils import gc_fraction
```


```python
my_seq = Seq("GATCGATGGGCCTATATAGGATCGAAAATCGC")
```


```python
# The sequence above is the same so the percentage will be the same. The function will import the decimal version 
gc_fraction(my_seq)
```




    0.46875




```python
# We can slice a sequence into multiple parts
my_seq[4:12]
```




    Seq('GATGGGCC')




```python
# We can do slices with a start and stop and a stride. The stride below starts at 0 and picks every third, skipping one and two.
my_seq[0::3]
```




    Seq('GCTGTAGTAAG')




```python
# This stride starts at 1, skips the next two, and picks the third.
my_seq[1::3]
```




    Seq('AGGCATGCATC')




```python
my_seq[2:3]
```




    Seq('T')




```python
# We can show the sequence backwards.
my_seq[::-1]
```




    Seq('CGCTAAAAGCTAGGATATATCCGGGTAGCTAG')




```python
# This brings the sequence back to a string 
str(my_seq)
```




    'GATCGATGGGCCTATATAGGATCGAAAATCGC'




```python
fasta_format_string = ">Name\n%s\n" % my_seq
```


```python
# This name will denote our fastest string
print(fasta_format_string)
```

    >Name
    GATCGATGGGCCTATATAGGATCGAAAATCGC
    



```python
seq1 = Seq("ACGT")
seq2 = Seq("AACCGG")
```


```python
seq1 + seq2
```




    Seq('ACGTAACCGG')




```python
seq2 + seq1
```




    Seq('AACCGGACGT')




```python
contigs = [Seq("ATG"), Seq("ATCCCG"), Seq("TTGCA")]
```


```python
spacer = Seq("N" *10)
```


```python
# This is taking the spacer (Ns) and joining it with the contigs above
spacer.join(contigs)
```




    Seq('ATGNNNNNNNNNNATCCCGNNNNNNNNNNTTGCA')

```python
dna_seq = Seq("acgtACGT")
```


```python
dna_seq
```




    Seq('acgtACGT')




```python
# This helps us if our sequence has both uppercase and lowercase. It converts the sequence making it easier.
dna_seq.upper()
```




    Seq('ACGTACGT')




```python
dna_seq.lower()
```




    Seq('acgtacgt')




```python
dna_seq.upper()
```




    Seq('ACGTACGT')




```python
# If you want to search you can do this.
"gtac" in dna_seq
```




    False




```python
# You need to save the sequence with the variable
dna_seq = dna_seq.upper()
```


```python
"GTAC" in dna_seq
```




    True




```python
my_seq = Seq("GATCGATGGGCCTATATAGGATCGAAAATCGC")
```


```python
# This will give you the complement to the original. 
my_seq.complement()
```




    Seq('CTAGCTACCCGGATATATCCTAGCTTTTAGCG')




```python
# This gives you the reverse complement of the original.
my_seq.reverse_complement()
```




    Seq('GCGATTTTCGATCCTATATAGGCCCATCGATC')




```python
protein_seq = Seq("EVRNAK")
protein_seq.complement()
```




    Seq('EBYNTM')




```python
coding_dna = Seq("ATGGCCATTGTAATGGGCCGATGAAAGGGTGCCCGATAG")
```


```python
coding_dna
```




    Seq('ATGGCCATTGTAATGGGCCGATGAAAGGGTGCCCGATAG')




```python
template_dna = coding_dna.reverse_complement()
```


```python
# This is the reverse complement which is basically how transcription works.
template_dna
```




    Seq('CTATCGGGCACCCTTTCATCGGCCCATTACAATGGCCAT')




```python
coding_dna
```




    Seq('ATGGCCATTGTAATGGGCCGATGAAAGGGTGCCCGATAG')




```python
# This will convert our Ts into Us. This step is a shortcut essentially. 
messenger_rna = coding_dna.transcribe()
```


```python
messenger_rna
```




    Seq('AUGGCCAUUGUAAUGGGCCGAUGAAAGGGUGCCCGAUAG')




```python
template_dna.reverse_complement().transcribe()
```




    Seq('AUGGCCAUUGUAAUGGGCCGAUGAAAGGGUGCCCGAUAG')




```python
# This turns the rna back to dna aka reverse transcription.
messenger_rna.back_transcribe()
```




    Seq('ATGGCCATTGTAATGGGCCGATGAAAGGGTGCCCGATAG')




```python
# RNA turns into protein.
messenger_rna.translate()
```




    Seq('MAIVMGR*KGAR*')




```python
# The asterisks indicate stop codons. 
```


```python
coding_dna.translate(table="Vertebrate Mitochondrial")
```




    Seq('MAIVMGRWKGAR*')




```python
# The sequence above does not have the premature stop codon like before because it was a mitochondrial gene.
```


```python
# You can do the same as above by using the codon tables and NCBI table numbers.
coding_dna.translate(table = 2)
```




    Seq('MAIVMGRWKGAR*')




```python
# This stops where there is a stop codon.
coding_dna.translate(to_stop = True)
```




    Seq('MAIVMGR')




```python
coding_dna.translate(table =2, to_stop=True)
```




    Seq('MAIVMGRWKGAR')




```python
# This replaces the asterisks with whatever symbol you want, in this case, an exclamation mark. 
coding_dna.translate(table = 2, stop_symbol = "!")
```




    Seq('MAIVMGRWKGAR!')




```python
gene = Seq("GTGAAAAAGATGCAATCTATCGTACTCGCACTTTCCCTGGTTCTGGTCGCTCCCATGGCAGCACAGGCTGCGGAAATTACGTTAGTCCCGTCAGTAAAATTACAGATAGGCGATCGTGATAATCGTGGCTATTACTGGGATGGAGGTCACTGGCGCGACCACGGCTGGTGGAAACAACATTATGAATGGCGAGGCAATCGCTGGCACCTACACGGACCGCCGCCACCGCCGCGCCACCATAAGAAAGCTCCTCATGATCATCACGGCGGTCATGGTCCAGGCAAACATCACCGCTAA")
```


```python
gene.translate(table = "Bacterial")
```




    Seq('VKKMQSIVLALSLVLVAPMAAQAAEITLVPSVKLQIGDRDNRGYYWDGGHWRDH...HR*')




```python
gene.translate(table = "Bacterial", to_stop = True)
```




    Seq('VKKMQSIVLALSLVLVAPMAAQAAEITLVPSVKLQIGDRDNRGYYWDGGHWRDH...HHR')




```python
# This tells biopython that this is a complete gene. Will make it start with methylene. It will also make sure it is a valid cds.
gene.translate(table = "Bacterial", cds = True)
```




    Seq('MKKMQSIVLALSLVLVAPMAAQAAEITLVPSVKLQIGDRDNRGYYWDGGHWRDH...HHR')




```python
from Bio.Data import CodonTable
```


```python
standard_table = CodonTable.unambiguous_dna_by_name["Standard"]
```


```python
mito_table = CodonTable.unambiguous_dna_by_name["Vertebrate Mitochondrial"]
```


```python
# This shows us our codon table.
print(standard_table)
```

    Table 1 Standard, SGC0
    
      |  T      |  C      |  A      |  G      |
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    T | TTT F   | TCT S   | TAT Y   | TGT C   | T
    T | TTC F   | TCC S   | TAC Y   | TGC C   | C
    T | TTA L   | TCA S   | TAA Stop| TGA Stop| A
    T | TTG L(s)| TCG S   | TAG Stop| TGG W   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    C | CTT L   | CCT P   | CAT H   | CGT R   | T
    C | CTC L   | CCC P   | CAC H   | CGC R   | C
    C | CTA L   | CCA P   | CAA Q   | CGA R   | A
    C | CTG L(s)| CCG P   | CAG Q   | CGG R   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    A | ATT I   | ACT T   | AAT N   | AGT S   | T
    A | ATC I   | ACC T   | AAC N   | AGC S   | C
    A | ATA I   | ACA T   | AAA K   | AGA R   | A
    A | ATG M(s)| ACG T   | AAG K   | AGG R   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    G | GTT V   | GCT A   | GAT D   | GGT G   | T
    G | GTC V   | GCC A   | GAC D   | GGC G   | C
    G | GTA V   | GCA A   | GAA E   | GGA G   | A
    G | GTG V   | GCG A   | GAG E   | GGG G   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--



```python
# Mitochondrial Table
print(mito_table)
```

    Table 2 Vertebrate Mitochondrial, SGC1
    
      |  T      |  C      |  A      |  G      |
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    T | TTT F   | TCT S   | TAT Y   | TGT C   | T
    T | TTC F   | TCC S   | TAC Y   | TGC C   | C
    T | TTA L   | TCA S   | TAA Stop| TGA W   | A
    T | TTG L   | TCG S   | TAG Stop| TGG W   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    C | CTT L   | CCT P   | CAT H   | CGT R   | T
    C | CTC L   | CCC P   | CAC H   | CGC R   | C
    C | CTA L   | CCA P   | CAA Q   | CGA R   | A
    C | CTG L   | CCG P   | CAG Q   | CGG R   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    A | ATT I(s)| ACT T   | AAT N   | AGT S   | T
    A | ATC I(s)| ACC T   | AAC N   | AGC S   | C
    A | ATA M(s)| ACA T   | AAA K   | AGA Stop| A
    A | ATG M(s)| ACG T   | AAG K   | AGG Stop| G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--
    G | GTT V   | GCT A   | GAT D   | GGT G   | T
    G | GTC V   | GCC A   | GAC D   | GGC G   | C
    G | GTA V   | GCA A   | GAA E   | GGA G   | A
    G | GTG V(s)| GCG A   | GAG E   | GGG G   | G
    --+---------+---------+---------+---------+--

```python
from Bio.Seq import MutableSeq
```


```python
mutable_seq = MutableSeq(my_seq)
```


```python
mutable_seq
```




    MutableSeq('GCCATTGTAATGGGCCGCTGAAAGGGTGCCCGA')




```python
# This will replace the fifth position in the sequence with a C.
mutable_seq[5] = "C"
```


```python
mutable_seq
```




    MutableSeq('GCCATCGTAATGGGCCGCTGAAAGGGTGCCCGA')




```python
mutable_seq.remove("T")
```


```python
mutable_seq
```




    MutableSeq('GCCACGTAATGGGCCGCTGAAAGGGTGCCCGA')




```python
mutable_seq.reverse()
```


```python
mutable_seq
```




    MutableSeq('AGCCCGTGGGAAAGTCGCCGGGTAATGCACCG')




```python
# If you wanted to change your gene and then go back to protection, you can do
new_seq = Seq(mutable_seq)
```


```python
new_seq
```




    Seq('AGCCCGTGGGAAAGTCGCCGGGTAATGCACCG')




```python
from Bio.Seq import reverse_complement, transcribe, back_transcribe, translate
```


```python
my_string = "GCTGTTATGGGTCGTTGGAAGGGTGGTCGTGCTGCTGGTTAG"
```


```python
reverse_complement(my_string)
```




    'CTAACCAGCAGCACGACCACCCTTCCAACGACCCATAACAGC'




```python
transcribe(my_string)
```




    'GCUGUUAUGGGUCGUUGGAAGGGUGGUCGUGCUGCUGGUUAG'




```python
back_transcribe(my_string)
```




    'GCTGTTATGGGTCGTTGGAAGGGTGGTCGTGCTGCTGGTTAG'




```python
translate(my_string)
```




    'AVMGRWKGGRAAG*'

### SEQUENCE ANNOTATIONS 

```python
from Bio.SeqRecord import SeqRecord
```


```python
from Bio.Seq import Seq
```


```python
simple_seq = Seq("GATC")
```


```python
# r stands for Record
simple_seq_r = SeqRecord(simple_seq)
```


```python
simple_seq_r
```




    SeqRecord(seq=Seq('GATC'), id='<unknown id>', name='<unknown name>', description='<unknown description>', dbxrefs=[])




```python
# We can give information for the unknowns
simple_seq_r.id = "AC12345"
```


```python
simple_seq_r.description = "made up sequence for the VDB Computational Biology Class"
```


```python
print(simple_seq_r.description)
```

    made up sequence for the VDB Computational Biology Class



```python
simple_seq_r.seq
```




    Seq('GATC')




```python
simple_seq_r
```




    SeqRecord(seq=Seq('GATC'), id='AC12345', name='<unknown name>', description='made up sequence for the VDB Computational Biology Class', dbxrefs=[])




```python
simple_seq_r.annotations["evidence"] = "None. This is just an example."
```


```python
# This is additional data.
print(simple_seq_r.annotations["evidence"])
```

    None. This is just an example.



```python
# The evidence does not show when you just do this.
simple_seq_r
```




    SeqRecord(seq=Seq('GATC'), id='AC12345', name='<unknown name>', description='made up sequence for the VDB Computational Biology Class', dbxrefs=[])




```python
# This is for sequencing. It will give scores for how confident the algorithm is when calling on a base. 
simple_seq_r.letter_annotations["phred_quality"] = [40, 40, 38, 30]
```


```python
print(simple_seq_r.letter_annotations)
```

    {'phred_quality': [40, 40, 38, 30]}

```python
# If you want to provide "fuzzy" information about genes you can do this. 
from Bio import SeqFeature
```


```python
# This means that we are saying the binding/repressor site etc will start somewhere after this position. 
start_pos = SeqFeature.AfterPosition(5)
```


```python
# This means that we think the end position will be somewhere between 9 but the left being 8 and the right being 9. 
end_pos = SeqFeature.BetweenPosition(9, left = 8, right = 9)
```


```python
my_location = SeqFeature.SimpleLocation(start_pos, end_pos)
```


```python
print(my_location)
```

    [>5:(8^9)]



```python
my_location.start
```




    AfterPosition(5)




```python
my_location.end
```




    BetweenPosition(9, left=8, right=9)




```python
# If you do not want all the other information, you can ask for the integer. 
int(my_location.end)
```




    9




```python
int(my_location.start)
```




    5




```python
exact_location = SeqFeature.SimpleLocation(5,9)
```


```python
print(exact_location)
```

    [5:9]



```python
exact_location.start
```




    ExactPosition(5)





    ['Project:58037']




```python
record.annotations.keys()
```




    dict_keys(['molecule_type', 'topology', 'data_file_division', 'date', 'accessions', 'sequence_version', 'gi', 'keywords', 'source', 'organism', 'taxonomy', 'references', 'comment'])




```python
# Since this is a circular genome, who is to say what the start and stop is. We can shift this. 
shifted = record[2000:] + record[:2000]
```


```python
shifted
```




    SeqRecord(seq=Seq('GATACGCAGTCATATTTTTTACACAATTCTCTAATCCCGACAAGGTCGTAGGTC...GGA'), id='NC_005816.1', name='NC_005816', description='Yersinia pestis biovar Microtus str. 91001 plasmid pPCP1, complete sequence', dbxrefs=[])




```python
len(shifted)
```




    9609




```python
# We haven't changed of the information, we only changed where we start in the circular chromosome.
```


```python
len(shifted.features)
```




    40




```python
shifted.annotations.keys()
```




    dict_keys(['molecule_type'])




```python
# There is none now in comparison to above.
shifted.dbxrefs
```




    []




```python
shifted.dbxrefs = record.dbxrefs[:]
```


```python
shifted.dbxrefs
```




    ['Project:58037']

```python
shifted.annotations = record.annotations.copy()
```

```python
shifted.annotations.keys()
```




    dict_keys(['molecule_type', 'topology', 'data_file_division', 'date', 'accessions', 'sequence_version', 'gi', 'keywords', 'source', 'organism', 'taxonomy', 'references', 'comment'])

```python
# The input above means: print the first value as a string, present the next four as integers, do that to the record id, and put length of each in integers.
## NC is the name; 9609 is the length; 41 features, 1 dbxref, and 13 annotations.
```

```python
rc = record.reverse_complement(id = "Testing")
```

```python
records = [
    rec.reverse_complement(id = "rc_" + rec.id, description = "reverse complement")
    for rec in SeqIO.parse("ls_orchid.fasta.txt", "fasta")
]
```


```python
len(records)
```




    94




```python
records = [
    rec.reverse_complement(id = "rc" + rec.id, description = "reverse complement")
    for rec in SeqIO.parse("ls_orchid.fasta.txt", "fasta")
    if len(rec) < 700
]
```


```python
len(records)
```




    18




```python
records = (
rec.reverse_complement(id = "rc_" + rec.id, description = "reverse complement")
for rec in SeqIO.parse("ls_orchid.fasta.txt", "fasta")
    if len(rec) < 700
)

SeqIO.write(records, "rev_comp.fasta", "fasta")
```




    18

```python
from Bio.Seq import Seq
from Bio.SeqRecord import SeqRecord
from Bio.Align import MultipleSeqAlignment
```


```python
align1 = MultipleSeqAlignment(
    [
            SeqRecord(Seq("ACTGCTAGCTAG"), id="Alpha"),
            SeqRecord(Seq("ACT-CTAGCTAG"), id="Beta"),
            SeqRecord(Seq("ACTGCTAGDTAG"), id="Gamma"),
        ]
    )

align2 = MultipleSeqAlignment(
    [
            SeqRecord(Seq("GTCAGC-AG"), id="Delta"),
            SeqRecord(Seq("GACAGCTAG"), id="Epsilon"),
            SeqRecord(Seq("GTCAGCTAG"), id="Zeta"),
        ]
    )

align3 = MultipleSeqAlignment(
    [
            SeqRecord(Seq("ACTAGTACAGCTG"), id="Eta"),
            SeqRecord(Seq("ACTAGTACAGCT-"), id="Theta"),
            SeqRecord(Seq("-CTACTACAGGTG"), id="Iota"),
        ]
    )
```


```python
my_alignments = [align1, align2, align3]
```


```python
my_alignments
```




    [<<class 'Bio.Align.MultipleSeqAlignment'> instance (3 records of length 12) at 7f0f3baa8b10>,
     <<class 'Bio.Align.MultipleSeqAlignment'> instance (3 records of length 9) at 7f0f3baa8c10>,
     <<class 'Bio.Align.MultipleSeqAlignment'> instance (3 records of length 13) at 7f0f3baa8d10>]




```python
print(my_alignments)
```

    [<<class 'Bio.Align.MultipleSeqAlignment'> instance (3 records of length 12) at 7f0f3baa8b10>, <<class 'Bio.Align.MultipleSeqAlignment'> instance (3 records of length 9) at 7f0f3baa8c10>, <<class 'Bio.Align.MultipleSeqAlignment'> instance (3 records of length 13) at 7f0f3baa8d10>]


```python
alignment = MultipleSeqAlignment(
    [
        SeqRecord(Seq("ACTCCTA"), id="seq1"),
        SeqRecord(Seq("AAT-CTA"), id="seq2"),
        SeqRecord(Seq("CCTACT-"), id="seq3"), 
        SeqRecord(Seq("TCTCCTC"), id="seq4"),
    ]
)
```


```python
print(alignment)
```

    Alignment with 4 rows and 7 columns
    ACTCCTA seq1
    AAT-CTA seq2
    CCTACT- seq3
    TCTCCTC seq4



```python
substitutions = alignment.substitutions
```


```python
print(substitutions)
```

        A    C    T
    A 2.0  4.5  1.0
    C 4.5 10.0  0.5
    T 1.0  0.5 12.0
    



```python
m = substitutions.select("ATCG")
```


```python
print(m)
```

        A    T    C   G
    A 2.0  1.0  4.5 0.0
    T 1.0 12.0  0.5 0.0
    C 4.5  0.5 10.0 0.0
    G 0.0  0.0  0.0 0.0
    



```python
m = substitutions.select("ACTG")
```


```python
print(m)
```

```python
E_VALUE_THRESH = 0.04
```


```python
for alignment in blast_record.alignments:
    for hsp in alignment.hsps:
        if hsp.expect < E_VALUE_THRESH:
            print ("****ALIGNMENT****")
            print("sequence:", alignment.title)
            print("length:", alignment.length)
            print("e value:", hsp.expect)
            print(hsp.query[0:75] + "...")
            print(hsp.match[0.75] + "...")
            print(hsp.sbjct[0.75] + "...")
```

### CHALLENGE 1 

```python
from Bio.Blast import NCBIWWW
```


```python
NCBIWWW.email = "enn003@email.latech.edu"
```


```python
from Bio import SeqIO
```


```python
record = SeqIO.read("sequence-2.fasta", format = "fasta")
```


```python
print(record)
```

    ID: XM_054372015.1
    Name: XM_054372015.1
    Description: XM_054372015.1 PREDICTED: Homo sapiens KRAS proto-oncogene, GTPase (KRAS), transcript variant X1, mRNA
    Number of features: 0
    Seq('CTAGGCGGCGGCCGCGGCGGCGGAGGCAGCAGCGGCGGCGGCAGTGGCGGCGGC...GAC')



```python
result_handle = NCBIWWW.qblast("blastn", "nt", record.seq, entrez_query = "Pan troglodytes")
```


```python
with open("blast_results.xml", "w") as out_handle:
    out_handle.write(result_handle.read())
result_handle.close()
```


```python
from Bio.Blast import NCBIXML
```


```python
result_handle = open("blast_results.xml")
```


```python
blast_record = NCBIXML.read(result_handle)
```


```python
E_VALUE_THRESH = 0.01
```


```python
for alignment in blast_record.alignments:
    for hsp in alignment.hsps:
        if hsp.expect < E_VALUE_THRESH:
            print ("****ALIGNMENT****")
            print("sequence:", alignment.title)
            print("length:", alignment.length)
            print("e value:", hsp.expect)

```

### OPEN CV BASICS 

```python
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt 
%matplotlib inline
```


```python
import cv2
```


```python
img = cv2.imread("puppy.jpg")
```


```python
type(img)
```




    numpy.ndarray




```python
img_wrong = cv2.imread('wrong/path/doesnot/abcdegh,jpg')
```


```python
type(img_wrong)
```




    NoneType




```python
# The image shows up blue becuase it codes for colors differently.
plt.imshow(img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6d8eb90>




![png](output_6_1.png)



```python
# This is how we will fix it.
fix_img = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
```


```python
plt.imshow(fix_img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6cb5710>




![png](output_8_1.png)



```python
# You can also make gray images. 
img_gray = cv2.imread("puppy.jpg", cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
img_gray.shape
```




    (1195, 1800)




```python
# The numbers above are the size of our image. 
```


```python
plt.imshow(img_gray)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6c2a450>




![png](output_11_1.png)



```python
# We need to transform it again for it to look correct. 
plt.imshow(img_gray, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6b9c690>




![png](output_12_1.png)



```python
# Grayscale images have less infomation in them. 
```


```python
# This is how we resize images. 
fix_img.shape
```




    (1195, 1800, 3)




```python
w_ratio = 0.5
h_ratio = 0.5

new_img = cv2.resize(fix_img, (0,0), fix_img, w_ratio, h_ratio)
```


```python
plt.imshow(new_img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6b25c90>




![png](output_16_1.png)



```python
new_img.shape
```




    (598, 900, 3)




```python
# You can flip images by doing this. 
flip_img = cv2.flip(fix_img, 0)
plt.imshow(flip_img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6b18490>




![png](output_18_1.png)



```python
# This image is flipped down. 
```


```python
flip_img2 = cv2.flip(fix_img, -1)
plt.imshow(flip_img2)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6a7f850>




![png](output_20_1.png)



```python
# This image is flipped down and backwards^
```


```python
type(fix_img)
```




    numpy.ndarray




```python
cv2.imwrite('puppy_fixed_image.jpg', flip_img)
```




    True




```python
img = cv2.imread("puppy.jpg")
```


```python
plt.imshow(img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a69f7690>




![png](output_25_1.png)



```python
img1 = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
```


```python
plt.imshow(img1)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6962710>




![png](output_27_1.png)



```python
# This will change the hue, saturation, and value of the image.
img2 = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2HSV)
```


```python
plt.imshow(img2)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a69548d0>




![png](output_29_1.png)



```python
# This will change hue, lightness, and saturation
img3 = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2HLS)
plt.imshow(img3)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a68bcbd0>




![png](output_30_1.png)



```python
img1 = cv2.imread("dnc.jpg")
img2 = cv2.imread("puppy.jpg")
```


```python
plt.imshow(img1)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a682cd90>




![png](output_32_1.png)



```python
img1 = cv2.cvtColor(img1, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
img2 = cv2.cvtColor(img2, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
```


```python
plt.imshow(img1)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a67a98d0>




![png](output_34_1.png)



```python
plt.imshow(img2)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a678f5d0>




![png](output_35_1.png)



```python
img1 = cv2.resize(img1, (1200,1200))
img2 = cv2.resize(img2, (1200,1200))
```


```python
alpha = 0.5 
beta = 0.5
```


```python
blended = cv2.addWeighted(img1, alpha, img2, beta, gamma=0)
```


```python
plt.imshow(blended)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a66fac50>




![png](output_39_1.png)



```python
# We were able to overwritten the two images. They are on top of each other with an equal amount of transparency.
```


```python
# Alpha is how transparent each level is. 
alpha = 0.8
beta = 0.2

blended1 = cv2.addWeighted(img1, alpha, img2, beta, 0)
```


```python
plt.imshow(blended1)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a66d8f50>




![png](output_42_1.png)



```python
alpha = 0.2
beta = 0.8

blended2 = cv2.addWeighted(img1, alpha, img2, beta, 0)

plt.imshow(blended2)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a66496d0>




![png](output_43_1.png)



```python
img1 = cv2.imread("dnc.jpg")
img2 = cv2.imread("puppy.jpg")

img1 = cv2.cvtColor(img1, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
img2 = cv2.cvtColor(img2, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

img1 = cv2.resize(img1, (200,200))
```


```python
large_img = img2
small_img = img1

x_offset = 0 
y_offset = 0

x_end = x_offset + small_img.shape[1]
y_end = y_offset + small_img.shape[0]

large_img[y_offset:y_end, x_offset:x_end] = small_img

plt.imshow(large_img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a65b2450>




![png](output_45_1.png)



```python
img = cv2.imread("rainbow.jpg")
```


```python
plt.imshow(img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a65934d0>




![png](output_47_1.png)



```python
# if we want to cancel out background colors. 
img = cv2.imread("rainbow.jpg", 0)

plt.imshow(img, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a64f7c50>




![png](output_48_1.png)



```python
# we will take this image and threshold it.
```


```python
ret1, thresh1 = cv2.threshold(img, 127, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

ret1
```




    127.0




```python
# 127 is nearly half the pixels. Everything above 127 and below will be changed to one color.
```


```python
plt.imshow(thresh1, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a645e8d0>




![png](output_52_1.png)



```python
# We can inverse the limits. 
img2 = cv2.imread("rainbow.jpg", 0)
ret1, thresh1 = cv2.threshold(img2, 127, 255, cv2.THRESH_TRUNC)

plt.imshow(thresh1, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a6442990>




![png](output_53_1.png)



```python
img3 = cv2.imread("rainbow.jpg", 0)
ret1, thresh1 = cv2.threshold(img3, 127, 255, cv2.THRESH_TOZERO)

plt.imshow(thresh1, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a43684d0>




![png](output_54_1.png)



```python
img_r = cv2.imread("crossword.jpg", 0)

plt.imshow(img_r, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f85a43446d0>




![png](output_55_1.png)



```python
def show_pic(img):
    fig = plt.figure(figsize = (15,15))
    ax = fig.add_subplot(111)
    ax.imshow(img, cmap = "gray")
    
show_pic(img_r)
```


![png](output_56_0.png)



```python
ret1, th1 = cv2.threshold(img_r, 127, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

show_pic(th1)
```


![png](output_57_0.png)



```python
ret1, th1 = cv2.threshold(img_r, 200, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

show_pic(th1)
```


![png](output_58_0.png)



```python
th2 = cv2.adaptiveThreshold(img_r, 255, cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_MEAN_C, cv2.THRESH_BINARY, 11, 8)

show_pic(th2)
```


![png](output_59_0.png)



```python
blended = cv2.addWeighted(src1 = th1, alpha = 0.6,
                         src2 = th2, beta = 0.4, gamma = 0)

show_pic(blended)
```


![png](output_60_0.png)



```python
th3 = cv2.adaptiveThreshold(img_r, 255, cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_MEAN_C, cv2.THRESH_BINARY, 11, 8)

blended = cv2.addWeighted(src1=th1, alpha=0.6,
                         src2=th2, beta=0.4, gamma=0)

show_pic(blended)
```


![png](output_61_0.png)



```python

```


### CORNER DETECTION

```python
import cv2
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline
```


```python
flat_chess = cv2.imread("greenchess.png")
flat_chess = cv2.cvtColor(flat_chess, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

plt.imshow(flat_chess)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c963cd90>




![png](output_1_1.png)



```python
gray_flat_chess = cv2.cvtColor(flat_chess, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
plt.imshow(gray_flat_chess, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c656a150>




![png](output_2_1.png)



```python
real_chess = cv2.imread("setchess.jpg")
real_chess = cv2.cvtColor(real_chess, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

plt.imshow(real_chess)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c64dc490>




![png](output_3_1.png)



```python
gray_real_chess = cv2.cvtColor(real_chess, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)

plt.imshow(gray_real_chess, cmap = "gray")
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c64ce150>




![png](output_4_1.png)



```python
gray = np.float32(gray_flat_chess)
dst = cv2.cornerHarris(src = gray, blockSize = 2, ksize = 3, k = 0.04)

dst = cv2.dilate(dst, None)
```


```python
flat_chess[dst>0.01*dst.max()] = [255, 0,0]

plt.imshow(flat_chess)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c4c2c290>




![png](output_6_1.png)



```python
gray = np.float32(gray_real_chess)
dst = cv2.cornerHarris(src = gray, blockSize = 2, ksize = 3, k = 0.04)

dst = cv2.dilate(dst, None)

real_chess[dst>0.01*dst.max()] = [255, 0,0]

plt.imshow(real_chess)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c4b94810>




![png](output_7_1.png)



```python
# Shi-Tomasi Corner Detection 
corners = cv2.goodFeaturesToTrack(gray_flat_chess, 64, 0.01, 10)
```


```python
corners = np.int0(corners)

for i in corners: 
    x,y = i.ravel()
    cv2.circle(flat_chess, (x,y), 3, (255, 0, 0), -1)
    
plt.imshow(flat_chess)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c4b852d0>




![png](output_9_1.png)



```python
corners = cv2.goodFeaturesToTrack(gray_real_chess, 100, 0.01, 10)

corners = np.int0(corners)

for i in corners: 
    x,y = i.ravel()
    cv2.circle(real_chess, (x,y), 3, (255, 0, 0), -1)
    
plt.imshow(real_chess)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb4c4aea650>




![png](output_10_1.png)



```python

```


### EDGE DETECTION 

```python
import cv2
import numpy as np 
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline
```


```python
img = cv2.imread("puppy.jpg")
plt.imshow(img)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a6487590>




![png](output_1_1.png)



```python
edges = cv2.Canny(image = img, threshold1 = 127, threshold2 = 127)

plt.imshow(edges)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a63c5390>




![png](output_2_1.png)



```python
med_value = np.median(img)
med_value
```




    97.0




```python
lower = int(max(0, 0.7*med_value))
upper = int(min(255, 1.3*med_value))

edges = cv2.Canny(img, threshold1 = lower, threshold2 = upper)

plt.imshow(edges)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a6343450>




![png](output_4_1.png)



```python
edges = cv2.Canny(image = img, threshold1 = lower, threshold2 = upper +100)

plt.imshow(edges)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a62b7390>




![png](output_5_1.png)



```python
blurred_img = cv2.blur(img, ksize = (5,5))

edges = cv2.Canny (image=blurred_img,
                  threshold1 = lower,
                  threshold2 = upper)

plt.imshow(edges)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a62238d0>




![png](output_6_1.png)



```python
blurred_img = cv2.blur(img, ksize = (5,5))

edges = cv2.Canny (image=blurred_img,
                  threshold1 = lower,
                  threshold2 = upper +50)

plt.imshow(edges)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a6193c90>




![png](output_7_1.png)



```python
blurred_img = cv2.blur(img, ksize = (5,5))

edges = cv2.Canny (image=blurred_img,
                  threshold1 = lower,
                  threshold2 = upper +100)

plt.imshow(edges)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7f22a6182fd0>




![png](output_8_1.png)



```python
blurred_img = cv2.blur(img, ksize = (7, 7))

edges = cv2.Canny (image=blurred_img,
                  threshold1 = lower,
                  threshold2 = upper +100)

plt.imshow(edges)
```

### OBJECT DETECTION 

```python
import cv2
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline
```


```python
full = cv2.imread("trainsunflower.jpeg")

full = cv2.cvtColor(full, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

plt.imshow(full)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fbe404ebcd0>




![png](output_1_1.png)



```python
test = cv2.imread("searchsunflower.jpg")

test = cv2.cvtColor(test, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

plt.imshow(test)
```




    <matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fbe3b8b04d0>




![png](output_2_1.png)



```python
print("test image shape:", full.shape)
print("training image shape:", test.shape)
```

    test image shape: (183, 275, 3)
    training image shape: (1080, 1920, 3)



```python
methods = ['cv2.TM_CCDEFF', 'cv2.TM_CCDEFF_NORMED', 'cv2.TM_CCDRR', 'cv2.TM_CCDRR_NORMED', 'cv2.TM_SQDIFF', 'cv2.TM_SQDIFF_NORMED']
```


```python
for m in methods: 
    
    test_copy = test.copy()
    method = eval(m)
    
    res = cv2.matchTemplate(test_copy, full, method)
    
    min_val, max_val, min_loc, max_loc = cv2.minMaxloc(res)
    
    if method in [cv2.TMSQDIFF, cv2.TM_SQDIFF_NORMED]:
        top_left = min_loc
    else: 
        top_left = max_loc
        
    height, width, channels = full.shape
    bottom_right = (top_left[0] + width, top_left[1] + height)
    
    cv2.rectangle(test_copy, top_left, bottom_right, (255,0,0),10)
    
    plt.subplot(121)
    plt.imshow(res)
    plt.title("Heatmap of template matching")
    plt.subplot(122)
    plt.imshow(test_copy)
    plt.title("Detection of template")
    
    plt.suptitle(m)
    
    plt.show()
    print('\n')
    print('\n')
```


    ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)

    <ipython-input-31-5105292a24f5> in <module>
          2 
          3     test_copy = test.copy()
    ----> 4     method = eval(m)
          5 
          6     res = cv2.matchTemplate(test_copy, full, method)


    <string> in <module>


    AttributeError: module 'cv2' has no attribute 'TM_CCDEFF'





    4.0




```python

```




    0.0





    4.0




